title: Dead Bush Overgrowth
description: Dead Bush Overgrowth brings the desert to life by replacing plain dead bushes with bones, skulls, and scattered remnants.
body:
## 🌵 Dead Bush Overgrowth
The desert shouldn’t feel empty—it should feel abandoned.

Dead Bush Overgrowth replaces plain dead bushes with a variety of atmospheric remnants such as bones, skulls, and scattered debris. These subtle details add environmental storytelling and visual depth to desert biomes while staying true to Minecraft’s vanilla aesthetic.

This pack is designed to feel natural, not modded—perfect for survival worlds, vanilla+ packs, and performance-focused setups.
## ✨ Features
- Multiple visual variants for dead bushes
- Includes bones, skulls, and desert remnants
- Vanilla-friendly and lightweight
- No gameplay changes—purely visual enhancement

## 🎯 Perfect For
- Vanilla+ modpacks
- Desert exploration builds
- Players who want more atmosphere without clutter

Make the desert feel alive—by showing what was left behind.

## 🧩 Dependencies
- No dependencies.

## 📝 Note
- If you want to use shaders and remove the swaying animation on bushes, open the .zip file of your shader pack, navigate to shaders/block.properties, and delete the line containing minecraft: dead_bush.

---
